en.extract:
The yellow-throated tinkerbird (Pogoniulus subsulphureus) is a species of bird in the Lybiidae family (African barbets). They are small and sedentary, and typically forage in the higher canopy of primary or established secondary forest. Yellow-throated tinkerbirds located closer to the coast, as well as those in louder environments, have been found to make higher-pitched vocalizations.


Range
It is found throughout the lowland intra-tropical rainforest of Sub-Saharan Africa.


Subspecies
Three subspecies are recognized, of which the westernmost taxon has the deepest yellow and starkest black plumage.

P. s. chrysopygus (Shelley, 1889) — Sierra Leone and southeastern Guinea eastward to southern Ghana
P. s. flavimentum (J.Verreaux & E.Verreaux, 1851) — Togo eastward through Nigeria, Cameroon, Gabon, Central African Republic, Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
P. s. subsulphureus (Fraser, 1843) — limited to Bioko Island


References


External links

Image at ADW
Yellow-throated tinkerbird (Pogoniulus subsulphureus) at The Internet Bird Collection

The '''yellow-throated tinkerbird''' ('''''Pogoniulus subsulphureus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the [[Lybiidae]] family (African barbets). They are small and sedentary, and typically forage in the higher canopy of primary or established secondary forest. Yellow-throated tinkerbirds located closer to the coast, as well as those in louder environments, have been found to make higher-pitched vocalizations.<ref name=":0">{{Cite bioRxiv |last1=Sebastianelli |first1=Matteo |last2=Blumstein |first2=Daniel T. |last3=Kirschel |first3=Alexander N. G. |date=2020-06-18 |title=Ambient noise from ocean surf drives frequency shifts in non-passerine bird song |biorxiv=10.1101/2020.06.17.156232}}</ref>

==Range==
It is found throughout the lowland<ref name=":0" /> intra-[[tropical forest|tropical rainforest]] of [[Sub-Saharan Africa]].

==Subspecies==
Three subspecies are recognized,<ref>{{cite web | doi=10.14344/IOC.ML.10.2 | title=IOC World Bird List 10.2 |url = http://www.worldbirdnames.org/DOI-10/master_ioc_list_v10.2.xlsx}}</ref> of which the westernmost taxon has the deepest yellow and starkest black plumage.<ref name="botw1">{{cite web |last1=Short |first1=L. L. |last2=Horne |first2=J. F. M. |title=Yellow-throated Tinkerbird (''Pogoniulus subsulphureus'') |url=https://doi.org/10.2173/bow.yettin1.01 |website=Birds of the World |publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology, Ithaca, NY, USA |access-date=20 April 2026 |ref=2020}}</ref>

* ''P. s. chrysopygus'' <small>(Shelley, 1889)</small> — Sierra Leone and southeastern Guinea eastward to southern Ghana
* ''P. s. flavimentum'' <small>(J.Verreaux & E.Verreaux, 1851)</small> — Togo eastward through Nigeria, Cameroon, Gabon, Central African Republic, Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
* ''P. s. subsulphureus'' <small>(Fraser, 1843)</small> — limited to [[Bioko Island]]
